title: HTTP Services, Clients, Boundaries, Security, and Graceful Shutdown · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A small service accepts hostile input, calls a slow dependency, serves concurrent requests, and must shut down without losing work. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a resilient secure HTTP service design while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design Handler and ServeMux boundaries, method and path semantics, request limits, response status, headers, and streaming order.
Reuse configured clients and transports, set request contexts and timeouts, close bodies, bound reads, and classify status separately from transport errors.
Validate untrusted input, escape output for its sink, minimize authority and data, protect secrets, and set explicit server resource limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Http Handler Routing
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Service request 5 has a hostile label, oversized body, slow dependency, duplicate header, and client cancellation during shutdown. Bound resources, validate and encode by sink, preserve stable responses, and drain owned work without leaking internals.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Http Handler Routing.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design handler and servemux boundaries, method and path semantics, request limits, response status, headers, and streaming order.
